title: Neurocognitive bases of self-monitoring of inner speech in hallucination prone individuals.

assets.abstract: Verbal hallucinations in schizophrenia patients might be seen as internal verbal productions mistaken for perceptions as a result of over-salient inner speech and/or defective self-monitoring processes. Similar cognitive mechanisms might underpin verbal hallucination proneness in the general population. We investigated, in a non-clinical sample, the cerebral activity associated with verbal hallucinatory predisposition during false recognition of familiar words -assumed to stem from poor monitori

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
